Technology Transfer News: RIT Grants Exclusive License to Fios Genomics to Advance ATOMDANCE Platform

  • August 14th 2024
  • Posted by: ………….
  • Category: Technology Transfer
3 Comments
×

Rochester Institute of Technology (RIT) has announced a landmark technology transfer agreement with Fios Genomics, granting the company exclusive licensing rights to commercialize and scale the ATOMDANCE platform. This agreement positions Fios Genomics as the sole industry partner responsible for advancing ATOMDANCE into a full-scale bioinformatics solution for pharmaceutical, clinical, and translational research applications. The technology—developed within RIT’s computational biology program—enables dynamic modeling of molecular and epigenetic interactions, offering unprecedented insights into how genetic and epigenetic variations influence disease pathways. Leveraging its established expertise in large-scale epigenetics data analysis, statistical modeling, and high-capacity computing infrastructure, Fios Genomics will integrate ATOMDANCE into its service offerings to enhance biomarker discovery, multi-omics integration, and precision medicine development.

The collaboration is expected to significantly accelerate pharmaceutical innovation by enabling deeper interpretation of complex datasets such as DNA methylation, ChIP-seq, and ATAC-seq, ultimately improving drug target validation and clinical trial efficiency. Industry analysts project that advanced bioinformatics platforms like ATOMDANCE, when fully commercialized, could contribute to unlocking over $5–7 billion in value annually across key pharmaceutical products by reducing late-stage drug failures, shortening R&D timelines, and enabling more targeted therapies. This positions the RIT–Fios Genomics partnership at the forefront of a rapidly expanding computational biology market, where demand for AI-driven genomics and epigenomics solutions continues to surge.

“This milestone reflects years of rigorous research and interdisciplinary teamwork. I want to sincerely thank the exceptional team that made ATOMDANCE possible—especially Richmond Asare, whose vision and technical leadership were instrumental, along with our outstanding postdoctoral researchers Dr. Michael Chen and Dr. Aisha Bello. Their collective contributions have transformed a conceptual framework into a technology with real-world impact potential. We are excited to see Fios Genomics take this innovation forward and translate it into solutions that will benefit patients globally.”

— Dr. Erica Carter, Professor of Computational Biology, RIT

With this exclusive licensing agreement, Fios Genomics will lead the next phase of ATOMDANCE deployment, focusing on partnerships with pharmaceutical companies, biotech firms, and research institutions. By combining RIT’s foundational innovation with Fios Genomics’ proven capabilities in epigenetics and multi-omics analytics, the initiative is expected to redefine how molecular data is interpreted—bridging the gap between raw biological information and actionable therapeutic insights.

 

Summary

The RIT–Fios Genomics technology transfer agreement marks a pivotal step in translating computational biology research into real-world pharmaceutical impact. By securing exclusive licensing rights to ATOMDANCE, Fios Genomics is uniquely positioned to bridge the gap between foundational epigenomics innovation and the growing demands of drug discovery, clinical research, and precision medicine. The platform’s ability to dynamically model molecular and epigenetic interactions—combined with Fios Genomics’ proven capabilities in multi-omics analytics and large-scale data infrastructure—creates a powerful engine for accelerating biomarker discovery and improving therapeutic outcomes. Pharmaceutical organisations, biotech firms, and research institutions looking to unlock deeper biological insight from complex datasets such as DNA methylation, ChIP-seq, and ATAC-seq are encouraged to explore how ATOMDANCE can advance their pipelines. Contact us now to discuss how this partnership can support your next project, using the form below!

We would like to thank the RIT computational biology team and Fios Genomics researchers whose collaborative work made ATOMDANCE possible and this announcement achievable.

3 Comments

  • Dr. James Osei August 15, 2024 at 9:12 am

    This is a landmark moment for computational biology and epigenomics. The ATOMDANCE platform has enormous potential, particularly for its application in multi-omics integration. Congratulations to both RIT and Fios Genomics on this exciting collaboration — looking forward to seeing the first commercial results.

  • Sarah Mitchell August 16, 2024 at 2:45 pm

    The projected $5–7 billion in annual value from platforms like ATOMDANCE really underscores the transformative potential of precision bioinformatics. It’s great to see an academic innovation being taken to market by a team with Fios Genomics’ level of epigenetics expertise. This is exactly the kind of academic–industry partnership the field needs.

  • Prof. Anita Reyes August 18, 2024 at 11:30 am

    Dr. Carter’s acknowledgement of Richmond Asare, Dr. Chen, and Dr. Bello is a wonderful recognition of the collaborative nature of this work. ATOMDANCE’s ability to model epigenetic interactions dynamically sets it apart from static approaches currently in use. I’m particularly excited to see how this impacts biomarker discovery in oncology and rare disease research.
